items.name: ARPA-E HORNIG
items.provides: Cost-shared R&D funding for design, fabrication, testing, modeling and qualification of transuranic (TRU) fuels for Gen IV reactors from recycled/used nuclear fuel and other strategic materials
items.deadline_label: Concept Paper deadline (2026-05-28/29) has passed; Full Application deadline not yet published (TBD)
items.fit: High — direct match to newcleo's closed fuel cycle, TRU/MOX fuel fabrication and Gen IV lead-cooled fast reactor fuel qualification focus
items.funds: Up to $50M total program funding announced; award sizes not yet confirmed for this specific NOFO
items.note: NOFO formally issued since last tracked (previously only a teaming-partner-list placeholder). Concept Paper Submission Deadline was 5/28-5/29/2026 ET and has now passed; Full Application deadline is not yet published on eXCHANGE. Standard ARPA-E domestic-operations/US-manufacturing conditions likely apply to a European-owned applicant.
items.change_note: NOFO (DE-FOA-0003623) has now been issued; Concept Paper deadline (2026-05-28) has passed; awaiting Full Application deadline announcement — previously tracked only as generically 'open' with no deadline.

items.name: NEUP
items.fit: Accessible only through a US university partner; fuel, materials and licensing R&D.
items.funds: University-led nuclear energy R&D awards (Nuclear Energy University Program).
items.note: Requires a US university PI. A partnership route, not a direct newcleo award.

items.name: NEUP (FY2026 CINR / CINR Phase II / DECP / Infrastructure Revitalization NOFOs)
items.sponsor: DOE Office of Nuclear Energy / INL (NEUP)
items.provides: Competitive R&D funding to US university-led teams (national labs and industry may only participate as subrecipients)
items.deadline_label: No open call currently — all FY2026 deadlines have passed (CINR-II LOI 2026-01-21, CINR-II Full App 2026-05-20, DECP 2026-03-10 (extended), main CINR Full App/SOW 2026-06-09); FY2027 cycle not yet announced
items.fit: Medium — newcleo cannot be prime applicant (university-only prime); relevant only as an industry subrecipient/partner on a university-led proposal
items.funds: Varies by NOFO track; historically individual CINR awards range from ~$5K to several million
items.note: All published FY2026 NEUP/CINR-family deadlines fall before the current date and have closed. No FY2027 solicitation dates have been posted yet on neup.inl.gov as of 2026-07-01; newcleo/newcleo Americas could only participate as an industry subrecipient to a US university lead, not as prime.
items.confidence: High (on closed dates); Low (on next-cycle timing)
items.change_note: All FY2026 NEUP solicitation deadlines previously open have now closed with no new deadline yet announced; status effectively 'no open call' pending FY2027 release.

items.name: GAIN NE Vouchers
items.fit: Direct access to national-lab capability for fuel fabrication, lead-cooled materials testing and licensing analysis.
items.funds: Access to DOE national-lab expertise and facilities at no cost; recipient provides 20% cost-share via a CRADA.
items.note: Recipients get lab access, not money. Eligibility ties resulting IP to US manufacturing: confirm newcleo Americas fit.

items.name: ARPA-E Advanced Reactor Fuels
items.provides: Teaming partner list only — facilitates team formation, no funding by itself
items.deadline_label: No deadline (teaming list); underlying potential NOFO appears superseded by HORNIG
items.fit: High (same TRU-fuels/domestic fissile resource scope as HORNIG)
items.note: The teaming-partner-list description (TRU fuels from domestic fissile resource, nuclear fuel independence) is essentially identical to HORNIG's scope. It is very likely this teaming list is the precursor that became the HORNIG NOFO (DE-FOA-0003623), and ARPA-E has simply left the teaming-list page live. Recommend consolidating tracking with 'ARPA-E HORNIG' going forward rather than treating as a separate opportunity.
items.change_note: Likely superseded/absorbed into the now-issued HORNIG NOFO; no separate NOFO has appeared under this name as of 2026-07-01.

items.name: Fuel-cycle resource FOAs
items.provides: Site access, used-fuel material, lab expertise
items.fit: Access to DOE sites, used-fuel material and national-lab expertise for fuel-cycle work.
items.funds: DE-FOA-0003611 and DE-FOA-0000001: provide site access, used-fuel material and lab expertise. Applicant carries every dollar.
items.note: Not capital. These are resource-access, applicant self-funds entirely. Confirm current status before acting.

items.name: NRIC Nuclear Energy Launch Pad (Launch Pad INL / Launch Pad USA)
items.sponsor: DOE / National Reactor Innovation Center (NRIC) at Idaho National Laboratory
items.kind: DOE authorization + site-access pathway (Request for Applications), rolling
items.provides: DOE Atomic Energy Act authorization (bypassing initial NRC licensing), pre-characterized federal land/site access, and technical assistance for reactor, fuel-fabrication, recycling or enrichment facilities; no cash — applicant funds all design, construction, operation and decommissioning
items.deadline_label: Initial-round submission deadline July 8, 2026 (5pm MDT); RFA remains open on a continual rolling basis thereafter, with further selections announced periodically
items.fit: High — covers fuel-fabrication and recycling facilities explicitly, and offers an alternative/complementary DOE authorization route to newcleo's Savannah River fuel-fab and LFR ambitions
items.funds: No direct funding; site parcels, DOE authorization pathway, and technical/regulatory assistance only
items.note: NRIC and DOE are establishing the Launch Pad to promote rapid private-industry deployment of advanced nuclear technologies, building on the Reactor and Fuel Line Pilot Programs. The RFA invites developers of reactors, enrichment facilities, fuel fabrication plants, and reprocessing/recycling operations to apply. Foreign-ownership review (relevant given newcleo Americas' European parent) can extend timelines — applicants have asked NRIC to clarify whether Strategic Partnership Project/OTA foreign-ownership review can begin in parallel with selection.

items.name: SPUR
items.fit: newcleo is already engaged via the Oklo partnership and the $2B US fuel-fabrication plan.
items.funds: Surplus plutonium (up to ~20 MT) for conversion to advanced-reactor fuel. Applicant carries all facility costs.
items.note: Five companies selected Oct 2025 (incl. Oklo); negotiations ongoing. Allocation of material, not cash.

items.name: CRADA
items.fit: Direct R&D partnership with a national lab (INL, ANL, ORNL, SRNL) on fuel and lead-cooled materials.
items.funds: Collaborative R&D: the lab brings expertise and facilities, the partner funds its own share. No cash to newcleo.
items.note: A mechanism, not a dated call. Can be initiated at any time, including under a GAIN voucher.

items.name: DOE Fuel Line Pilot Program
items.sponsor: U.S. DOE (Office of Nuclear Energy), authorization-based RFA
items.kind: DOE authorization pathway for advanced nuclear fuel fabrication lines (non-NRC)
items.provides: DOE authorization to build/operate a fuel fabrication line outside national laboratories, intended to unlock private funding and fast-track future NRC licensing; no cash
items.deadline_label: No fixed deadline confirmed; DOE has run at least two selection rounds (2025 and mid-2026) and continues to make selections as capacity allows
items.fit: Medium-high — Oklo (newcleo's Savannah River MOX partner) has already used this exact mechanism for fuel-fabrication facilities, illustrating the pathway newcleo Americas could also use for its own facility
items.funds: No direct funding; each company is responsible for all costs associated with construction, operation, and decommissioning of their fuel fabrication facilities, and must manage sourcing of nuclear material feedstock
items.note: Oklo was selected to build and operate three fuel fabrication facilities to support its Aurora and Pluto reactors, and possibly other fast reactors. Not yet confirmed whether a new open call exists for additional entrants beyond the two rounds already run.

items.name: DOE Surplus Plutonium Utilization Program (SPUR)
items.sponsor: DOE Office of Nuclear Energy (in coordination with NNSA-managed surplus plutonium stocks)
items.kind: Material allocation program (RFA-based selection, now in negotiated agreements)
items.provides: Access to designated surplus (former weapons-program) plutonium — up to 20 metric tons potentially available — for conversion into fuel for advanced reactors; DOE provides material, industry funds all processing/fabrication costs
items.deadline_label: No open application deadline — five companies already selected for advanced negotiations (announced 2026-05-26); RFA originally issued 2025-10-21
items.fit: Very High — newcleo is directly involved via its Oklo partnership; this is the clearest live US federal pathway matching newcleo's surplus-plutonium/MOX and Savannah River-adjacent strategy
items.funds: No cash; DOE makes surplus plutonium material available as a 'bridge fuel' resource; recipient company bears all conversion, safeguards and fabrication costs
items.note: On 2026-05-26, Oklo announced it was one of five companies (with Exodys Energy, SHINE Technologies, Standard Nuclear and Flibe Energy) selected by DOE for advanced negotiations under this program. Oklo is partnering explicitly with newcleo, which would contribute fuel expertise and potential project capital; newcleo is not itself the DOE counterparty. Selection is for advanced negotiations only — not yet a definitive, binding agreement, and is subject to further security/safeguards approvals.
items.change_note: Status confirmed/clarified: publicly announced 2026-05-26 that Oklo (in partnership with newcleo) is one of five companies selected by DOE for advanced negotiations — this fills in sponsor, mechanism, and partner detail for the previously bare-bones tracked 'SPUR' entry (status 'selected' unchanged, but now substantiated with an official, dated source).

items.name: ARPA-E NEWTON
items.fit: Used-fuel transmutation R&D, lab-led and already awarded.
items.funds: Transmutation technology R&D. Awards made in 2025 (Argonne, Fermilab and others).

items.name: ARDP
items.fit: The large demonstration money, effectively committed to the X-energy and TerraPower cohort.
items.funds: Advanced Reactor Demonstration Program cost-share for first-of-a-kind builds.
items.note: Reference line, not a live opportunity for newcleo.
